title: betula × caerulea
intro: Betula × caerulea, also known as the blue birch or river birch, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the crossing of Betula papyrifera (paper birch) and Betula nana (dwarf birch). The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Betula × caerulea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
